body.u: My grandchildren were playing in the garden I looked out the windows, they were playing games I once had played. I sat in the chair and thought my last day at school. I left May 26th 1978 at 16. I joined Toyota GB LtD as a junior office clerk on Tuesday 30th May I can remember sitting at home scared stiff up at half past 6 so not to overlay (did not start till nine, with a five minute walk). In 1980 had been promoted to an clerk, doing wages etc. That year I also met Jim at the works party (Christmas).
Enjoyed the work more and more, now girls had joined us, I had to take them under my wing I knew how they felt.
1981 Brother married I was chief bridesmaid I also got engaged. I applied for office secretary but turned down.
1982 my 21st had a large party and cried when my mum gave me a silver watch from her and dad, I cried again (with happiness when Jim proposed I accepted.

The first few months of married life were hard, our social life was virtually nil, as we saved as much as we goodcould. The day of the wedding was 14 May 1982. We stayed on the Island at Rushenden but Jim changed his job. 1984 I was a mother I can remember my mums face when I told her she was going to be a nanny. It was spoilt by her and dad a lot.
1986 I was a mother again I had left work for the first child but never gone back, told I could go if I wanted to.
I waited till the children started school. Jamie the first child cried he didn’t want to leave, so when it was Claire’s turn I was prepared, only she didn’t and went off happily.
1984 Also was mum and dads 25th wedding anniversary, sister-in-law and I had prepared a huge tea for them and a presantpresent.
1986 started back to work to fit in times with the children.
The children grew up quickly and I can remember their first words still.
1990 it was our eigtheighth anniversary and the children brought us breakfast in bed cold coffee and bread and marmalade (I smile as I sit I my chair) that first Christmas was hectic.

The year 2000 dad died of a heart attack it broke my heart and mums, she came to live with us but a few years later she died as she was to lonely on her own and couldn’t live without him (As I remember this tears come into my eyes but the children come in and put my mind at rest).
